instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on 8 Unknown Facts to Know about Term Insurance - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Body Content:
We all want what’s best for the people we love. A life insurance policy helps people achieve this goal, even in their absence. Among the various types of insurance policies available in the market, term insurance plans happen to be one of the most popular choices due to their affordable structuring. With a term plan, you can opt for the maximum sum assured against relatively low premium rates. This makes them popular life cover options for people.
If you’re thinking about securing the future of your family with a term plan, here are 8 unknown facts you should keep in mind:
1.   Simple and Cost-Effective Life Insurance Options <H2>
A term insurance plan is a life insurance policy that offers you a comprehensive umbrella of coverage against a pocket-friendly premium rate.  Such plans offer a high sum assured, against relatively competitive premiums.
Term plans cover the insured for a certain stipulated period and the death benefit from the plan is only payable to the nominee if the policyholder passes away during this duration. If the insured is alive when the term period ends, no maturity benefits are extended. However, the best term insurance plans now give policyholders the option to get back the entire premium amount when the plan matures.
2.   You can Extend the Coverage of your Term Plan With Add-Ons <H2>
Your term insurance plan does not just take care of your loved ones in your absence, but it can also come in handy when trying to avoid financial losses due to certain unpredictable eventualities. For instance, if you are diagnosed with a critical illness (CI) and have opted for a Critical illness cover, your insurer will pay you the sum assured under this rider. This will help mitigate some of the financial burdens associated with such illnesses and their treatments.
Moreover, if you have a Waiver of Premium cover in place, you’ll also be exempted from premium payments once you’ve been diagnosed with any one of the listed CIs in your plan. Accidental Death Benefit and Accidental Total Permanent Disability riders are other common add-ons that help make the coverage of term plans more comprehensive.
3.   The Older You Get, the More Premiums You’ll Have to Pay <H2>
While 65 years is the upper age limit for most term covers, buying one when you’re young makes the most sense as the premium costs of such plans tend to increase with age. Thus, by insuring your life with a term plan early on, you get to enjoy prolonged coverage benefits against relatively reduced premium rates.
4.   Term Plans have Flexible Tenures <H2>
Term plans don’t have fixed tenures. While most such plans mature in 30 to 35 years, you can opt for ones that come with shorter or longer tenures, as per your insurance requirements and life goals. Keeping this in mind you can opt for plans with a 5-year tenure or ones that come with more extensive tenures.
5.   You Can Customise Term Covers <H2>
When buying a term insurance plan, you can customise your coverage to match your life goals and insurance needs. For instance, you wouldn’t want your absence to hinder the educational aspirations of your kids.
To this end, you can opt for a term plan that comes with an extra education cover to ensure that your children get the best education without worrying about the growing expenses associated with the same. Similarly, by opting for joint life term plans, you can safeguard the life of your spouse and yourself with a single term policy. Thus, the choices are endless when it comes to picking a term plan.
6.   Premium Payments Can Be Made As Per Your Convenience  <H2>
Apart from customising the tenure and features of your term plan, you can also customise premium payment intervals as per your convenience. For instance, if you are a salaried employee, you can opt for a regular pay term plan, where you need to make monthly, quarterly or yearly premiums throughout your policy tenure.
For those with a more fluctuating source of income, a limited pay term plan may be better suited. A single premium plan, where you pay the premium all at once is suitable for those with surplus funds.
7.   Term Plans have a High Claim Settlement Ratio <H2>
When looking to secure the financial future of your family in your absence, you would only want to opt for the best. This is why choosing life insurance policies with high claim settlement ratios is important. You wouldn’t want your loved ones to go through a complicated claim settlement process and then have the claim rejected. This is where term plans become the most prudent choice as along with comprehensive coverage, term insurance plans also come with a high claim settlement ratio.
8.   You Can Buy a Term Insurance Plan in Seconds <H2>
Buying a term insurance plan doesn’t take long. You can buy an online term plan in a matter of seconds. Buying an online term plan also helps cut down on intermediary costs, thus making it a more lucrative option to consider. You can also compare policy coverage and costs when looking for a term cover online. This makes the process of finding the best term insurance plan exceptionally simple and hassle-free. But before you start shortlisting policies, remember to research and correlate them with your life goals.
Wrapping It Up <H2>
By picking a term cover, you ensure that the financial well-being of your family is taken care of, even in your absence. Since term insurance plans are flexible and customisable, you can tweak them to ensure that your family keeps reaching its life goals even when you’re not around to provide them with a financial safety net.

output.final_text:
8 Lesser Known Facts about Term Insurance We all want what’s best for the people we love. A life insurance policy helps people achieve this goal, even in their absence. Among the various types of insurance policies available in the market, term insurance plans happen to be one of the most popular choices due to their affordability . With a term plan, you can opt for the maximum sum assured against relatively low premium rates. This makes them popular life cover options for people. If you’re thinking about securing the financial future of your family with a term plan, here are 8 lesser known facts you should keep in mind: 1. Simple and Cost-Effective Life Insurance Options A term insurance plan is a life insurance policy that offers you a comprehensive umbrella of coverage against a pocket-friendly premium rate. Such plans offer a high sum assured, against relatively competitive premiums. Term plans cover the insured for a certain stipulated period and the death benefit from the plan is only payable to the nominee if the life assured passes away during this duration. If the insured is alive when the term period ends, no maturity benefits are extended. However, the term insurance plans with return of premium option gives policyholders the option to get back the entire premium amount when the plan matures. 2. You can Extend the Coverage of your Term Plan with Add-Ons Your term insurance plan does not just take care of your loved ones in your absence, but it can also come in handy when trying to avoid financial losses due to certain unpredictable eventualities. For instance, if you are diagnosed with a critical illness (CI) and have opted for a Critical illness rider, your insurer will pay you the sum assured under this rider as per the underlying rider conditions. This will help mitigate some of the financial burdens associated with such illnesses and their treatments. Moreover, in some cases, if you have a Waiver of Premium rider in place, you’ll also be exempted from premium payments if you’ve been diagnosed with any one of the listed CIs in your plan. Accidental Death Benefit and Accidental Total Permanent Disability riders are other common add-ons that help make the coverage of term plans more comprehensive. 3. The Older You Get, the More Premiums You’ll Have to Pay While 65 years is the upper age limit for most term covers, buying one when you’re young makes the most sense as the premium costs of such plans tend to increase with age. Thus, by insuring your life with a term plan early on, you get to enjoy prolonged coverage benefits against relatively reduced premium rates.
4. Term Plans have Flexible Tenures Term plans have many options when it comes to how long you want to cover yourself. While most such plans mature in 30 to 35 years, you can opt for ones that come with shorter or longer tenures, as per your insurance requirements and life goals. Keeping this in mind you can even opt for plans with a 5-year tenure or ones that come with more extensive tenures.
5. You Can Customise Term Covers
When buying a term insurance plan, you can customise your coverage to match your life goals and insurance needs. For instance, you wouldn’t want your absence to hinder the educational aspirations of your kids.
You can opt for a term plan that comes with an extra education cover to ensure that your children get the best education without worrying about the growing expenses associated with the same. Similarly, by opting for joint life term plans, you can safeguard the life of your spouse and yourself with a single term policy. Thus, the choices are endless when it comes to picking a term plan. 6. Premium Payments Can Be Made as Per Your Convenience Apart from customising the tenure and features of your term plan, you can also customise premium payment intervals as per your convenience. For instance, if you are a salaried employee, you can opt for a regular pay term plan, where you need to make monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or yearly premium payments throughout your policy tenure. For those with a more fluctuating source of income, a limited pay term plan may be better suited. A single premium plan, where you pay the premium all at once is suitable for those with surplus funds. 7. Claim Settlement Ratio When purchasing life insurance, you must consider several factors before determining which provider is suitable for you. You must learn about the insurance company's numerous policies and coverage options. You must understand how their users rate their services and customer relationship management. Many individuals, on the other hand, overlook the importance of checking the claim settlement ratio, which may be a significant factor in choosing a suitable term plan. 8. You Can Buy a Term Insurance Plan in a few minutes Buying a term insurance plan doesn’t take long. You can buy an online term plan in a matter of minutes. Buying an online term plan also helps cut down on intermediary costs, thus making it a more lucrative option to consider. You can also compare policy coverage and costs when looking for a term cover online.
This makes the process of finding the suitable term insurance plan exceptionally simple and hassle-free. But before you start shortlisting policies, remember to research and correlate them with your life goals.
Wrapping It Up
By picking a term cover, you ensure that the financial well-being of your family is taken care of, even in your absence. Since term insurance plans are flexible and customisable, you can tweak them to ensure that your family keeps reaching its life goals even when you’re not around to provide them with a financial safety net.
